back_translation: All the sons of Israel went out, and the congregation assembled as one man, from Dan to Beersheba and the land of Gilead, before the LORD at Mizpah.
The leaders of all the people, all the tribes of Israel, stood in the assembly of the people of God — 400,000 foot soldiers who drew the sword.
The sons of Benjamin heard that the sons of Israel had gone up to Mizpah. And the sons of Israel said, 'Speak, how did this evil happen?'
The Levite man, husband of the murdered woman, answered and said, 'I came to Gibeah which belongs to Benjamin — I and my concubine — to lodge.'
'The lords of Gibeah rose up against me and surrounded the house at night because of me. They intended to kill me, and my concubine they humbled, and she died.'
'So I took my concubine and cut her up and sent her throughout all the territory of the inheritance of Israel, because they had done lewdness and folly in Israel.'
'Behold, all of you sons of Israel, give your word and counsel here.'
All the people rose up as one man, saying, 'None of us will go to his tent, and none of us will turn to his house.'
'And now this is the thing we will do to Gibeah: against her by lot.'

'We will take ten men out of a hundred from all the tribes of Israel, and a hundred out of a thousand, and a thousand out of ten thousand, to take provisions for the people, that they may act when they come to Gibeah of Benjamin, according to all the folly that they did in Israel.'
All the men of Israel gathered against the city as one man, united together.
The tribes of Israel sent men throughout all the tribe of Benjamin, saying, 'What is this evil that has come about among you?'

'And now give over the men, the sons of Belial, who are in Gibeah, that we may put them to death and purge the evil from Israel.' But Benjamin would not listen to the voice of their brothers the sons of Israel.
The sons of Benjamin gathered from the cities to Gibeah, to go out for war with the sons of Israel.
The sons of Benjamin were mustered that day from the cities — 26,000 men who drew the sword, besides 700 chosen men of the inhabitants of Gibeah.
Out of all this people were 700 chosen men, left-handed, every one of them slinging a stone at a hair and not missing.
The men of Israel were mustered, apart from Benjamin — 400,000 men who drew the sword, all of these men of war.
They rose and went up to Bethel and inquired of God, and the sons of Israel said, 'Who shall go up for us first for the war with the sons of Benjamin?' And the LORD said, 'Judah first.'
The sons of Israel rose in the morning and camped against Gibeah.
The men of Israel went out for war with Benjamin, and the men of Israel arrayed with them for the battle at Gibeah.
The sons of Benjamin came out from Gibeah, and they destroyed in Israel that day 22,000 men to the ground.
The people, the men of Israel, strengthened themselves and again arrayed for battle in the place where they had arrayed on the first day.

The sons of Israel went up and wept before the LORD until evening, and they inquired of the LORD, saying, 'Shall I again draw near for war with the sons of Benjamin my brother?' And the LORD said, 'Go up against him.'
The sons of Israel drew near to the sons of Benjamin on the second day.
Benjamin came out against them from Gibeah on the second day, and they destroyed in the sons of Israel again 18,000 men to the ground, all of these drawers of the sword.

All the sons of Israel and all the people went up and came to Bethel, and they wept and sat there before the LORD, and they fasted that day until evening, and they offered up burnt offerings and peace offerings before the LORD.
The sons of Israel inquired of the LORD — and there was the ark of the covenant of God in those days.

And Phinehas son of Eleazar son of Aaron was standing before it in those days, saying, 'Shall I again go out for war with the sons of Benjamin my brother, or shall I cease?' And the LORD said, 'Go up, for tomorrow I will give him into your hand.'
Israel set ambushes against Gibeah round about.
The sons of Israel went up against the sons of Benjamin on the third day and arrayed against Gibeah as time after time.

The sons of Benjamin came out against the people; they were drawn from the city. They began to strike from the people slain as time after time, on the roads — one going up to Bethel and one to Gibeah in the field — about thirty men in Israel.
The sons of Benjamin said, 'They are struck before us as at the first.' But the sons of Israel said, 'Let us flee and draw them from the city to the roads.'
All the men of Israel rose from their place and arrayed at Baal-tamar, and the ambush of Israel was breaking out from its place, from the open land of Geba.
Ten thousand chosen men from all Israel came against Gibeah, and the battle was heavy, but they did not know that disaster was touching them.
The LORD struck Benjamin before Israel, and the sons of Israel destroyed in Benjamin that day 25,100 men, all of these drawers of the sword.
The sons of Benjamin saw that they were defeated, and the men of Israel gave place to Benjamin because they trusted in the ambush they had set against Gibeah.
The ambush hastened and burst against Gibeah, and the ambush spread out and struck all the city with the edge of the sword.
Now the appointed signal between the men of Israel and the ambush was their causing to go up a great rising of smoke from the city.
The men of Israel turned in the battle, and Benjamin had begun to strike slain among the men of Israel about thirty men, for they said, 'Surely they are struck before us as in the first battle.'
The signal began to rise up from the city, a pillar of smoke, and Benjamin turned behind him, and behold, the whole of the city went up to the heavens.
The men of Israel turned, and the men of Benjamin were dismayed, for they saw that disaster had touched them.
They turned before the men of Israel toward the way of the wilderness, but the battle overtook them, and those from the cities destroyed them in their midst.
They surrounded Benjamin, pursued him, trampled him down without rest, opposite Gibeah, from the rising of the sun.
There fell from Benjamin 18,000 men, all of these men of valor.

They turned and fled toward the way of the wilderness to the rock of Rimmon, and they gleaned from him on the roads 5,000 men, and they followed closely after him as far as Gidom, and they struck from him 2,000 men.
All who fell from Benjamin were 25,000 men drawing the sword that day, all of these men of valor.
Six hundred men turned and fled toward the way of the wilderness to the rock of Rimmon, and they dwelt at the rock of Rimmon four months.

The men of Israel turned back against the sons of Benjamin and struck them with the edge of the sword — from the city of every gathered one even to the beast even to all that was found — even all the cities found they sent into fire.
